source.title: CPIC Guideline for Thiopurine Dosing Based on TPMT and NUDT15
source.finding: TPMT intermediate: reduce thiopurine dose to 30-80%. Poor: consider alternative or 10% dose.

CC.effect: No TPMT activity. Life-threatening myelosuppression with standard thiopurine doses.
CC.recommendation: Reduce dose to 10% of standard or use alternative. Mandatory monitoring.

CT.effect: Reduced TPMT activity. Increased risk of myelosuppression.
CT.recommendation: Reduce thiopurine dose to 30-80% of standard. Monitor CBC weekly at initiation.

TT.recommendation: Standard thiopurine dosing with routine monitoring.

TT.effect: TPMT*3B homozygous. No TPMT activity. Life-threatening myelosuppression with standard thiopurine doses.
TT.recommendation: Reduce dose to 10% of standard or use alternative. Mandatory monitoring.

CT.effect: TPMT*3B heterozygous. Reduced TPMT activity. Increased risk of myelosuppression.
CT.recommendation: Reduce thiopurine dose to 30-80% of standard. Monitor CBC weekly at initiation.

TC.effect: TPMT*3B heterozygous. Reduced TPMT activity. Increased risk of myelosuppression.
TC.recommendation: Reduce thiopurine dose to 30-80% of standard. Monitor CBC weekly at initiation.

CC.effect: No TPMT*3B variant. Normal TPMT activity.
CC.recommendation: Standard thiopurine dosing with routine monitoring.

CG.recommendation: Reduce thiopurine dose to 30-80% of standard. Monitor CBC weekly at initiation.

CC.effect: No TPMT*2 variant. Normal TPMT activity.
